result.summary: The central allowlist refactor strongly improves path handling by eliminating direct filesystem sinks (like .resolve()) on request-controlled full paths. It correctly decomposes paths lexically, enforces least authority for overlapping roots by failing closed without fallback, and implements a robust, fail-closed SARIF gate for CI.
coverage: Traversal and symlink-escape resistance (improved via decomposition and `resolve_secure_path`)
Overlapping-root behavior and least authority (correctly enforced by sorting and hard-failing on canonical check)
File vs directory contracts (correctly extracted to `validate_directory`)
Cross-platform path handling (safe use of `os.path` and `Path`, strict `.` and `..` segment rejection)
Raw CodeQL SARIF gate (robust, fails closed on missing/malformed output, catches undetected alerts)
Regressions in `_find_repos` (removed code was safely unreachable)
residual_risks: Lexical matching relies on exact string prefix equivalence, which may fail safe (reject valid paths) on case-insensitive filesystems (Windows/macOS) if the user provides a path with different casing than the registered root.
